name: ADAM Framework
applicability: Closest architectural cousin per CREDITS.md. Two specific scripts named: coherence_monitor.py reads Claude Code JSONL session files and detects compaction events; nightly_reconciliation.py is end-of-day automation that produces a morning brief. Direct analogs to JASON-OS compact-restore hook + the absent /session-end-followed-by-/session-begin handoff. Worth a future /repo-analysis pass.
home_connection: 5-layer persistent AI memory and identity framework. Different audience but same Claude Code substrate. ADAM is where the firings-log + decay analysis pattern likely originated.

name: unmutable/ai-chief-of-staff
applicability: Same repo name, different author. April-2026 wave. Worth a Quick Scan if JASON-OS wants a comparison on personal-OS shape variants.
home_connection: None direct. Reference for shape comparison.

name: kbanc85/claudia
applicability: Claude Code session management and skill layer per CREDITS.md. Session-management overlap with JASON-OS is direct. Quick Scan candidate.
home_connection: Skill layer structure could echo JASON-OS .claude/skills/ pattern.

name: MemPalace
applicability: April-2026 'wings, halls, and rooms' memory architecture per CREDITS.md. Memory architecture with spatial framing — different mental model than directory-of-typed-files. Could surface assumptions JASON-OS auto-memory makes by contrast.
home_connection: Comparison fodder; not a port target.

name: Hermes Agent (Nous Research)
applicability: Earlier predecessor; named for lineage. Self-improving agent with persistent memory.
home_connection: None direct.

name: Andrej Karpathy LLM Wiki gist
applicability: April-2026 markdown-folder-maintained-by-LLM. Validates the broader markdown-plus-AI direction. Useful framing reference if JASON-OS produces public positioning material.
home_connection: Conceptual lineage. Worth reading for landscape framing.

name: Obsidian Dataview
applicability: SQL-like query layer over markdown files. Pattern: 'treat markdown as a database without leaving markdown.' Even though JASON-OS isn't Obsidian, the *shape* (declarative queries against a corpus of typed markdown files with frontmatter) is exactly what JASON-OS auto-memory could grow toward if/when entries justify it. Inspirational, not a port.
home_connection: Demonstrates that markdown-as-substrate scales further than ad-hoc grep.

name: Obsidian Smart Connections
applicability: Local embedding + per-note vector for semantic search. Pattern: 'add a vector layer over your typed-markdown corpus for cross-domain queries.' If JASON-OS auto-memory or .research/ output ever needs cross-source synthesis beyond keyword match, this is the prior art for how to do it locally.
home_connection: Pattern (local-embedding semantic layer over markdown), not the implementation.

name: Obsidian MCP Tools
applicability: HTTP endpoint inside the editor + MCP server binary that talks to it. Pattern: 'expose your local editor as an MCP server so external AI can query it like a tool.' JASON-OS could expose its own .research/ index or skill registry the same way if/when external tools need programmatic access.
home_connection: MCP-as-glue pattern.

name: Obsidian Templater
applicability: Note templates that execute small scripts. Pattern: 'templates that aren't just text fill but can run logic on creation.' JASON-OS skills could have analogous template-with-logic pattern when scaffolding new skills/research/state files.
home_connection: Inspirational shape for any future skill/state scaffolding.

name: Obsidian CLI
applicability: Editor exposes its API as a CLI: search, eval, backlinks, unresolved-links, tasks. Pattern: 'every reader/editor should be scriptable from the shell.' JASON-OS doesn't have a unified CLI but several skills have implicit shapes that could converge. The author's hard-won lesson — 'I spent weeks doing MCP gymnastics that the CLI would have replaced in a single command' — is a pure tool-selection insight.
home_connection: Tool-tier discipline (cheaper tool first) directly applies.

name: Search vs read structural argument
applicability: AI working through search-API-mediated workspace degrades on cross-source synthesis; AI with direct file-system access handles it. Articulates JASON-OS's existing filesystem-verification posture in tenet-shaped language. Could be lifted as a JASON-OS tenet ('AI reads, doesn't search') alongside the existing filesystem-verification tenet.
home_connection: Direct tenet candidate. Not currently named in JASON-OS.

name: Format-longevity / plain-text-outlives-the-tool
applicability: Plain-text markdown is readable in any text editor in 30 years; proprietary databases aren't. Asymmetric migration pain (Notion→Obsidian clean, Obsidian→Notion lossy). JASON-OS already lives this implicitly (markdown everywhere) but doesn't articulate the principle.
home_connection: Tenet candidate. Worth surfacing alongside in-house-over-handoff and conversational-explanatory.

name: Business-model-as-architecture
applicability: Author argues local-first architecture is a business-model choice (no servers to pay for) which produces no exit pressure which produces no incentive to monetize-your-data. JASON-OS has no commercial framing today; the *discipline* of asking 'what does the funding model imply for the architecture?' is the takeaway.
home_connection: Framing reference. Not currently a JASON-OS concern but worth knowing.

name: Use-both / bridge-via-Claude-MCP
applicability: Personal layer in tool A + collaborative layer in tool B + Claude reading both via MCP. The *pattern* (split layers by use-context, bridge with AI reading both) maps onto JASON-OS's potential SoNash-app-infra + JASON-OS-portable-layer + cross-repo-movement-reframe split. Same shape, different specifics.
home_connection: Direct architectural analog. Worth keeping in mind for the reframe plan.

name: Tool-cost ranking + cheapest-tool-first rule
applicability: Author ranks reach-the-vault options by cost: raw file read < CLI shell exec < eval-via-CLI < MCP server. 'Most MCP servers load all their tool schemas into context at the start of the conversation, whether or not you call any of them.' Rule: 'raw reads for known paths, CLI for search and listings, semantic for cross-domain only, MCP only when you need rendered view.' JASON-OS already has ToolSearch for deferred tools; the *principle* of cheapest-tool-first deserves articulating.
home_connection: Tenet candidate. Maps onto JASON-OS's existing ToolSearch deferred-tool pattern but is broader (covers raw Read vs Bash vs MCP vs Skills).

name: MCP-tax framing
applicability: MCP servers cost tokens before any call. The cost is paid up-front in tool-schema loading. JASON-OS users see this in the form of large MCP server lists at session start. Knowing the framing is the first step to deciding which MCPs earn their place.
home_connection: JASON-OS environment shows this exact pattern (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Sentry MCPs loaded). Worth knowing when deciding to add more.

name: Don't-batch-install MCPs
applicability: 'Add these one at a time. Verify each one works before adding the next. Never batch-install MCP servers and hope they all come up.' Generalizes to skill-porting, hook-adding, dependency-introduction.
home_connection: JASON-OS already follows this implicitly via per-skill port discipline. Worth surfacing as a stated rule.

name: Semantic-vs-keyword-search heuristic
applicability: 'Use keyword for specific strings/names/technical terms. Use semantic for themes/concepts/vibes.' Decide *before* running anything. Currently low-applicability (JASON-OS uses ripgrep almost everywhere); becomes relevant if/when JASON-OS adds an embedding layer.
home_connection: Decision-discipline pattern. Inspirational shape.

name: Cross-domain filtering pattern
applicability: Wrap semantic-search call in helper that excludes results from same top-level folder as the source — surfaces cross-domain connections instead of nearest-neighbor noise. Translatable to any indexed-corpus query.
home_connection: Pattern reference for any future cross-source synthesis in JASON-OS.

name: Hook-shape pattern
applicability: SessionStart, Stop, PreToolUse hooks registered in .claude/settings.json. JASON-OS uses Node.js hooks; this repo uses Python hooks. Same pattern, different language. Validates JASON-OS's existing hook architecture and shows it works across language choices. Specific hooks named — date injection (we have an analog via UserPromptSubmit), session audit, coherence monitor, /wrap-reminder Stop hook.
home_connection: Validates existing pattern; coherence-monitor is the hook idea worth examining.

name: ADAM coherence_monitor.py
applicability: Reads Claude Code JSONL session files; detects compaction events; writes alerts. ai-chief-of-staff adapted it (removed SENTINEL daemon, added compaction detection). JASON-OS has compact-restore.js as part of its session-start hook. Pattern is to MONITOR session JSONL for state changes — directly applicable to JASON-OS hook layer expansion.
home_connection: Direct JASON-OS pattern: read .claude session JSONL, detect events, fire side-actions. Worth a future /repo-analysis on ADAM itself.

name: ADAM nightly_reconciliation.py
applicability: End-of-day automation producing morning brief. JASON-OS has /session-end → SESSION_CONTEXT.md but not the cross-day reconciliation step. Worth knowing about; not blocking.
home_connection: Conceptual analog for any future overnight automation.

name: CREDITS.md as a maintained protocol
applicability: Written rules for when attribution gets added, what per-file docstrings must contain, the asymmetry between concept-adapted (lighter requirement) and code-adapted (full MIT copyright + URL + path). JASON-OS has a SoNash lineage that the whole repo embodies. The *protocol shape* for handling that attribution is worth examining — does JASON-OS need its own CREDITS.md as patterns flow back/forth between SoNash and JASON-OS?
home_connection: Direct pattern for managing JASON-OS ↔ SoNash cross-pollination.

name: Adjacent-implementations listing
applicability: Naming peer projects (with rationale per project) so 'the field is named rather than strawmanned.' JASON-OS deep-research outputs already cite sources; a discrete 'adjacent implementations' framing for any public-facing positioning would borrow this discipline.
home_connection: Inspirational shape for future research-output framing.

name: Doc-readable-by-human-and-LLM-coach
applicability: README explicitly designed to work two ways: human reads it as install steps, OR human pastes it into Claude and Claude walks them through. obsidian-setup.md has 'For the human reading this' section followed by 'Below the line is Claude's operating manual.' JASON-OS docs aren't currently dual-mode; the pattern is interesting if JASON-OS ever produces public-facing docs.
home_connection: Style pattern for future user-facing documentation.

name: docs/laws.md
applicability: Four operating laws with rationale and copy-pasteable instruction blocks. Direct relevance to JASON-OS Behavioral Guardrails. Two converged (Operating Constraint, Verification Protocol). Two crisply-phrased (Scope Discipline, Execute-Don't-Meta-Talk).
home_connection: JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4 has 16 guardrails; ai-chief-of-staff has 4. Compactness contrast is teaching.

name: docs/memory.md
applicability: Decay-and-provenance section: firings log + decay analysis + 10-session bootstrap gate. JASON-OS auto-memory has none of this.
home_connection: Direct candidate when memory churn becomes a problem.

name: docs/feedback-loop.md
applicability: Rule-why-apply feedback memory format (already converged). Rubber-stamp warning is the additional insight worth absorbing.
home_connection: Direct pattern; rubber-stamp warning is port-now.

name: docs/protocols.md
applicability: State-file contracts. Two-tier user-readable vs _system/ split.
home_connection: Worth knowing for any future user-facing JASON-OS surface.

name: ARCHITECTURE.md
applicability: Long-form essay. Lineage section is honest about evolutionary path. Style reference.
home_connection: Style for any future JASON-OS narrative documentation.

name: smart_search.py
applicability: Wraps Obsidian CLI for semantic + keyword search. Code-quality reference: subprocess timeouts, json.dumps escaping for embedded JS, proper exit codes, env-var override for paths. Tiny utility done right.
home_connection: Code-craft reference. JASON-OS scripts/lib/ utilities follow similar discipline; this validates the pattern.
